speaker: Lisa Su
summary: AMD announced Zen 5, a ground-up CPU core design for PCs, data centers, and supercomputers. The core doubles several internal bandwidth paths versus Zen 4 and is positioned as AMD's highest-performance, most energy-efficient core to date.
quote: All of this comes together in the product in Ryzen 9000 series, and we're delivering an average of 16% higher IPC across a broad range of application benchmarks and games compared to Zen 4.

speaker: Lisa Su
summary: AMD announced the Ryzen 9000 desktop CPU family for the AM5 platform, bringing Zen 5 to consumer desktops with PCIe 5 and DDR5 support. The lineup includes 16-, 12-, 8-, and 6-core versions aimed at enthusiast and mainstream desktop segments.
quote: Now, in addition to the top of the stack, Ryzen 9950X, we're also announcing the 12-, 8-, and 6-core versions that will bring the leadership performance of Zen 5 to mainstream price points, and all of these go on sale in July.

speaker: Lisa Su
summary: AMD introduced the Ryzen 9 9950X as the flagship Ryzen 9000 desktop processor. It is a 16-core Zen 5 consumer CPU with high boost clocks and a 170W TDP, positioned as the fastest consumer CPU in the world.
quote: We have 16 Zen 5 cores, 32 threads, up to 5.67 gigahertz boost, a large 80 megabyte cache at 170 watt TDP.

speaker: Lisa Su
summary: AMD committed to supporting the AM5 desktop socket through 2027 and beyond. This extends AMD's platform-longevity strategy and gives desktop OEMs and enthusiasts visibility into future CPU upgrade paths.
quote: And we're taking this exact same strategy with Socket AM5, which we now plan on supporting through 2027 and beyond, so you're going to see AM5 processors from us for many, many years to come.

speaker: Lisa Su
summary: AMD announced its third-generation Ryzen AI 300 series, codenamed Strix, for ultra-thin and premium notebooks. The chip integrates Zen 5 CPU cores, RDNA 3.5 graphics, and an XDNA 2 NPU for Copilot+ AI PCs.
quote: We have a new NPU that delivers an industry-leading 50 TOPS.

speaker: Lisa Su
summary: AMD announced the flagship Ryzen AI 9 HX 370 mobile processor within the Ryzen AI 300 family. It combines 12 Zen 5 cores, 24 threads, cache, integrated NPU capability, and RDNA graphics for premium AI notebooks.
quote: The flagship Ryzen 9 HX 370 has 12 Zen 5 cores, 24 threads, 36 megabytes of cache, the industry's most integrated NPU, and our latest RDNA graphics.

speaker: Lisa Su
summary: AMD announced that XDNA 2 is the first NPU to support block FP16 data types. This is positioned as enabling generative AI models to run with 16-bit-like accuracy and 8-bit-like performance without quantization compromises.
quote: Now XDNA 2 is the first NPU to support block 16-bit floating-point, and what that means is block FP16 actually combines the accuracy of 16-bit data with the performance of 8-bit data.

speaker: Lisa Su
summary: AMD committed to expanding the software ecosystem for Ryzen AI and other AMD AI PC platforms. The company said it is on track to have more than 150 ISVs developing for AMD AI platforms by the end of 2024.
quote: And by the end of 2024, we're on track to have more than 150 ISVs developing for AMD AI platforms across content creation, consumer, gaming, and productivity applications.

speaker: Lisa Su
announcement: HP next-generation OmniBook with Ryzen AI 300
summary: HP announced a next-generation OmniBook using AMD's Ryzen AI 300 series. The system is positioned as an AI PC leveraging the 50 TOPS NPU for local generative AI and real-time AI experiences.
quote: This is the next generation OmniBook, when we integrate the latest Ryzen AI 300 series that as Lisa was saying before, will be the first product that will have 50 TOPS integrated in the device.

speaker: Lisa Su
summary: Lenovo announced plans to launch AI laptops using AMD third-generation Ryzen AI processors across Yoga, ThinkPad, and ThinkBook lines. Lenovo also highlighted exclusive on-device AI features such as Creator Zone and AI Now.
quote: So later this year, we are going to launch Lenovo AI laptops with the third-gen Ryzen AI processors for consumers through our Yoga franchisee, for commercial with our legendary ThinkPad brand, and for small and medium businesses through our ThinkBook lineup.

speaker: Lisa Su
announcement: ASUS Ryzen AI 300 AI PC lineup
summary: ASUS announced a broad AI PC lineup powered by AMD third-generation Ryzen AI processors, spanning ZenBook, ProArt, Vivobook, ASUS TUF, and ROG laptops. ASUS said it would make third-gen Ryzen AI systems available for purchase in July.
quote: I think later today at 4 p.m. we'll be unveiling a range of cutting-edge AI PCs across our portfolio with brand new ZenBook, ProArt, Vivobook, ASUS TUF, and ROG laptops powered by the 3rd Gen AMD Ryzen AI processors.

speaker: Lisa Su
announcement: Versal AI Edge Gen 2 early access
summary: AMD announced early access for the next-generation Versal AI Edge Gen 2 platform. It integrates pre-processing, inferencing, and post-processing into a single adaptive chip for edge AI devices.
quote: More than 30 of our strategic partners are already developing Edge AI devices powered by our new single-chip Versal solution, and we are incredibly excited about the opportunity to drive AI at the Edge and see significant opportunities to extend our embedded market leadership with these new technologies.

speaker: Lisa Su
summary: AMD previewed its 5th Gen EPYC processor, codenamed Turin, for data center servers. The CPU features up to 192 cores, supports current memory and IO standards, and is designed as a drop-in replacement for existing 4th Gen EPYC platforms.
quote: Turin features 192 cores and 384 threads and has 13 different chiplets built in 3 and 6 nanometer process technology.

speaker: Lisa Su
summary: Stability AI announced that Stable Diffusion 3 Medium will be released for public download. The model is described as an optimized SD3 version that can run on MI300 and is compact enough for Ryzen AI laptops.
quote: Today we're announcing that on June 12th we will release the Stable Diffusion 3 medium model for everyone to download.

speaker: Lisa Su
summary: AMD announced the Instinct MI325X accelerator as the next product in its annual data-center GPU cadence. It adds faster HBM3E memory and is designed to use the same infrastructure as MI300 for easier customer transition.
quote: MI325 extends our leadership in generative AI with up to 288 gigabytes of ultra-fast HBM3E memory with 6 terabytes per second of memory bandwidth, and it uses the same infrastructure as MI300, which makes it easy for customers to transition.

speaker: Lisa Su
summary: AMD announced the Instinct MI350 series for 2025, based on the new CDNA 4 architecture. The accelerator family will use 3nm process technology, support FP4 and FP6 data types, and target a large generational AI performance uplift.
quote: And with CDNA 4, we're on track to deliver 35 times increase.

speaker: Lisa Su
summary: AMD committed to a 2026 Instinct MI400 series using a new CDNA Next architecture. This extends AMD's data-center GPU roadmap beyond MI325X and MI350 on an annual cadence.
quote: And then in 2026, we'll deliver another brand-new architecture with CDNA next in the MI400 series.

speaker: Lisa Su
summary: AMD announced progress toward the Ultra Accelerator Link open scale-up interconnect standard. UA Link is positioned as an open alternative to proprietary accelerator fabrics, with the 1.0 standard targeted for later in 2024 and chips already in development.
quote: The UA Link 1.0 standard is on track for later this year, with chips supporting UA Link already well into development from multiple vendors.

speaker: Rene Haas
summary: Arm announced Armv9 as the next major Arm architecture milestone, spanning CPUs from microcontrollers to servers. It is positioned as the secure foundation for the next decade of Arm-based compute.
quote: recently we announced arm v9 the next major milestone for the arm architecture for the decade ahead arm v9 will apply across all our cpus from those smallest microcontrollers to the largest server-based designs

speaker: Rene Haas
summary: Arm introduced Confidential Compute Architecture as part of the Armv9 roadmap to protect code and data while in use. It adds hardware-backed realms separated from secure and non-secure worlds, targeting stronger data protection across devices and infrastructure.
quote: the rmv9 roadmap introduces the arm confidential compute architecture this technology shields portion of code and data from access or modification while in use even from privileged software by performing computation in a hardware-based secure environment

speaker: Rene Haas
announcement: Memory Tagging Extensions in first-generation Armv9 CPUs
summary: Arm said Memory Tagging Extensions, developed with Google, will be supported in first-generation Armv9 CPUs. The feature is intended to uncover memory vulnerabilities before exploitation and is slated to appear in devices the following year.
quote: support of memory tagging extensions are an integral part of the first generation of arm v9 cpus available in devices next year

speaker: Rene Haas
summary: Arm launched its latest Total Compute solutions as a blueprint combining Arm CPUs, GPUs, software, tools, physical IP and standards for client systems. The platform is intended to improve performance, efficiency and system scalability across premium, performance and efficiency tiers.
quote: now last week we launched our latest manifestation of total compute solutions and our first cortex cpu is on arm v9

speaker: Rene Haas
summary: Cortex-X2 is Arm’s first Armv9 flagship CPU from the Cortex-X custom program. It targets ultimate performance for premium client devices and is cited as delivering higher performance than Cortex-X1 on the same process and frequency.
quote: cortec x2 is our first v9 flagship cpu for ultimate performance from the cortex custom x program x2 delivers another 16 improvement performance compared to cortex x1 running on the same process at the same frequency

speaker: Rene Haas
summary: Cortex-A710 is Arm’s first Armv9 big CPU. It emphasizes energy efficiency gains over Cortex-A78 for next-generation client SoCs.
quote: cortex a710 it's our first v9 big cpu with a remarkable 30 energy efficiency gain over our previous generation cortex a78

speaker: Rene Haas
summary: Cortex-A510 is Arm’s first new little core in four years. It targets smartphones, home devices and wearables with higher performance in a low-power profile.
quote: now our first new little core in four years is the cortex a510 which delivers an impressive 35 performance improvement compared to the previous generation cortex 855 making it our most capable little core ever

speaker: Rene Haas
announcement: Scalable Dynamic Shared Unit for Armv9 CPU clusters
summary: Arm announced a new scalable Dynamic Shared Unit to support high-performance Armv9 CPU clusters. It enables designs with up to eight Cortex-X2 cores while maintaining an efficiency-oriented architecture.
quote: for uncompromised performance the v9 cpu cluster is now supported by a brand new scalable dynamic shared unit this allows a maximum of eight cortex x2 cores while still staying true to arm's dna and that's efficiency

speaker: Rene Haas
summary: Mali-G710 is Arm’s highest-performing GPU to date, aimed at flagship and premium smartphones and Chromebooks. It brings higher graphics performance and machine-learning uplift versus the prior generation.
quote: mali g710 is the highest performing gpu from arm ever aimed at flagship and premium smartphones in the growing chromebook market it delivers a 20 performance improvement and 35 ml uplift over the previous generation

speaker: Rene Haas
summary: Arm announced Mali-G510 and Mali-G310 GPUs for performance and ultra-efficient client segments. They target devices ranging from digital TVs and set-top boxes to VR, AR and wearables.
quote: mali g510 and mali g310 bring new features and technologies to the performance and ultra efficient segments powering premium graphic experiences from a wide range of devices from digital tvs and subject boxes through vr ar and wearables

speaker: Rene Haas
summary: Neoverse V1 is Arm’s first platform in a new performance-computing tier for edge-to-cloud infrastructure. It targets vector, machine-learning and high-CPU-performance workloads while preserving SoC design flexibility.
quote: neoverse v1 is the first in a new performance computing tier delivering significant uplifts in vector and machine learning along with high cpu performance while still providing the soc design flexibility they require

speaker: Rene Haas
summary: Neoverse N2 is the first Neoverse platform based on Armv9, targeting infrastructure workloads with better security, power efficiency and performance. Arm highlighted a single-threaded performance gain versus Neoverse N1 at similar power and area efficiency.
quote: the neoverse n2 platform is the first based on the arm v9 with improvements to security power efficiency and performance it delivers 40 percent higher single threaded performance compared to n1 at the same level of power and area efficiency

speaker: Rene Haas
summary: Arm said Neoverse N2 is the first platform to feature SVE2, an Armv9 capability for cloud-to-edge performance efficiency. It targets workloads including machine learning, DSP, multimedia and 5G systems with improved programmability and portability.
quote: now neoverse n2 is also the first platform to feature sve2 an rmv9 feature that drives large improvements in cloud to edge performance efficiency for a broader set of use cases like machine learning digital signal processing multimedia and 5g systems

speaker: Rene Haas
announcement: NVIDIA Grace Arm-based data center CPU
summary: Arm highlighted NVIDIA Grace as NVIDIA’s first Arm-based data-center CPU for high-performance computing workloads. The CPU is positioned to deliver a large performance improvement versus current fastest servers on complex HPC workloads.
quote: grace is nvidia's first arm-based data center cpu it will deliver 10x to performance of today's fastest server running some of the most complex high-performance computing workloads

speaker: Rene Haas
summary: Arm described a new NVIDIA Arm HPC Developer Kit combining Ampere Altra CPUs, NVIDIA A100 GPUs and NVIDIA BlueField-2 DPUs. Taiwan’s National Center for High Performance Computing was named among initial deployment sites.
quote: and finally a new nvidia arm hpc developer kit this kit includes an ampere ultra cpu which contains 80 neoverse cores running up to 3.3 gigahertz dual nvidia a100 gpus each of those delivering 312 teraflops of floating point deep learning ml performance and two nvidia bluefield 2 dpus

speaker: Rene Haas
announcement: MediaTek-NVIDIA AI thin-and-light laptop reference platform
summary: Arm said MediaTek and NVIDIA announced a specialized reference platform for AI-enabled thin-and-light laptops. The platform signals ecosystem work around Arm-based client PCs with AI acceleration.
quote: our long-time partner mediatek have been delivering specialized processing solutions for many years in the mobile space and recently they partnered with nvidia to announce a specialized reference platform for an ai enabled thin and light laptop

speaker: Rene Haas
summary: Sunplus is developing an SoC for autonomous robotic industrial applications using Arm Cortex-A CPU, Mali GPU and Ethos neural processors. The design shows Arm IP being combined for specialized endpoint AI systems.
quote: sunplus is developing an soc that combines an arm cortex acpu molle gpu and our ethos neural processors this soc has been designed specifically for autonomous robotic industrial applications

speaker: Rene Haas
announcement: Himax next-generation always-on smart camera AI SoC
summary: Himax is working on a next-generation AI SoC for always-on smart cameras based on Cortex-M55 and Ethos-U55. The product targets low-power endpoint AI vision applications.
quote: hi-max is working on their next generation aisoc uniquely designed for always-on smart cameras that's based on the cortex m55 and ethos u55 solution i mentioned earlier

speaker: ASUS launch
summary: A new ultra-slim 16-inch AI PC in the Zenbook line using AMD Ryzen AI 300, positioned as a flagship everyday Copilot+ PC-class laptop with local AI performance and premium ceramic-aluminum chassis. It is available for preorder immediately through ASUS channels.
quote: all of this and even more Mark our brand new Zen book series the flagship of our series and aiming our performance with third generation ryzen AI processor means it's significantly faster in so many different way over previous gen and M3 fer GPU CPU 50 trillion top soft performance more memory bandwidth more pixel outlet for all types of AI and creativity task and all this Freedom comes to you for the price starting from today us 1,39 ey available for pre-order on usb.com and worldwide Asus store starting from today

speaker: ASUS launch
summary: A redesigned TUF Gaming A16 laptop with Ryzen AI 9 HX-class silicon, Copilot+ PC readiness, and higher total system power for gaming and AI workloads. The thermal redesign is positioned to support higher performance while lowering skin temperature versus the prior generation.
quote: our T gaming a16 can support higher power limits up to 170 watts of total system performance power in manual modes and this generation of tough gaming a16 is significantly faster compared to the previous gen of ryzen I 89 45h

speaker: ASUS launch
summary: A new compact 14-inch TUF Gaming laptop aimed at portable gaming, pairing Ryzen AI 9 HX-class CPU/NPU capability with GeForce RTX discrete graphics. ASUS emphasized its small size and high combined CPU/GPU power budget for the form factor.
quote: the tough A14 is incredibly compact weights only just a 1.46 kilogram but despite it size it delivered impressive performance typical laptop as size usually handles a 30 wat CPU even if you would have a discrete graphics it's low powered

speaker: ASUS launch
summary: ASUS expanded the ProArt monitor lineup with the 5K PA27JCV, a creator display with high pixel density, factory calibration, DCI-P3 color coverage, USB-C power delivery, and KVM features. ASUS gave an explicit Q3 availability target.
quote: please anticipate the 5K Po pa27 jcv in Q3

speaker: ASUS launch
summary: ASUS announced the ProArt PA32QCV 6K monitor as part of its expanded ProArt display lineup for creators needing more workspace and color-accurate high-resolution panels. The 6K model offers substantially more workspace than 4K according to ASUS.
quote: the 5K monitor offers 77% more workspace than 4K and the 6K monitor offers 145% more with these large canvases you can effortlessly navigate through your creative content without constant zooming enabling greater efficiency in your work

speaker: ASUS launch
summary: A new 16-inch ProArt creator laptop with Ryzen AI 9 HX processor, up to 64GB memory, GeForce RTX 4070 GPU, 4K Lumina OLED touchscreen, and creator-focused software bundles. It is positioned as a high-performance AI laptop for creative workloads and is available for preorder later the same day.
quote: our PO p16 with latest ryzen AI 9hx processor up to 64 gig of memory and the RTX 4070 GPU was start fun 1899 and the px13 was St found $699 available to pre-order on asus.com and Best Buy later today

speaker: ASUS launch
summary: A new convertible ProArt creator laptop with Ryzen AI 9 HX-class silicon and a 3K Lumina OLED display, designed for flexible sketching, editing, and mobile creative work. It is part of the new ProArt AI PC lineup and was slated for preorder later the same day.
quote: the pra px13 offer unbounded versatility with its convertible design whether you are sketching on a go on a go or editing high resolution videos design for creators who demands flexibility without compromise on quality a vibrant 3K Lumina OLED brings your creativity to life with cinematic quality

speaker: ASUS launch
summary: A rugged detachable ProArt tablet powered by Qualcomm Snapdragon X Series, targeting portable Copilot+ PC creative use with local NPU AI features, an OLED display, bundled keyboard/kickstand, and all-day battery life. ASUS said it will be available in the second half.
quote: powered by the latest Qualcomm Snapdragon X Series with up to 45 tops mpu the pr pc13 effess lights up the copilot plus PC experience and it comes with an Ample Storage of one terabytes so it will be able to save hundreds of high definition videos and thousands of high definition photos bringing all your creative contents at your fingertip

speaker: ASUS launch
summary: ASUS announced MuseTree, an in-house AI ideation tool for creators that generates and refines concepts through idea maps and sketch-based canvases. It runs locally on ProArt laptops, emphasizing offline AI creativity without subscription fees.
quote: I'm very excited to be here to share with you two Innovative software tools today M Tre and story cube both develop inh housee and capable of taking full advantages of the high performing Pro laptops

speaker: ASUS launch
summary: ASUS announced StoryCube, an AI-powered media file manager for organizing photos and videos across devices by map, timestamp, and AI-recognized scenarios. The tool uses NPU acceleration to categorize creative assets locally.
quote: story cube is your go-to AI power file manager to keep all your precious memories organized and within reach

speaker: ASUS launch
summary: ASUS announced a ProArt partnership with CapCut, including free premium membership and ASUS Dial shortcut integration for the video editor. The deal strengthens ASUS’s creator software bundle around AI-assisted video editing.
quote: now one more exciting news not to be missed today we're thrilled to announce our partnership between capot and pro art together to elevate your creative journey

speaker: ASUS launch
summary: ASUS committed that all ProArt laptops will include Adobe Creative Suite and CapCut Pro trial bundles, lowering the entry cost for creator AI software workflows. This is a concrete bundling commitment tied to the new ProArt laptop family.
quote: all the pr laptops will include a three month Adobe Creative suits and a six-month of cap cup Pro for free saving you hundreds of dollars to start experiencing the AI superpower in Creative tools

speaker: ASUS launch
summary: ASUS announced a Blackwell-based AI server portfolio covering NVIDIA GB200 NVL72, HGX B100, HGX B200, and MGX GB200 NVL2 systems. This expands ASUS server offerings into NVIDIA’s newest AI data-center platform generation.
quote: this computex assus is announcing blackw based servers for both the gb200 NV 72 the hgx B100 the hgx b200 as well as the mgx gb200 nbl2

speaker: ASUS launch
announcement: ASUS ESC AI POD with NVIDIA GB200 NVL72
summary: ASUS introduced the ESC AI POD rack solution using NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 for trillion-token large language model training and real-time inference. This is a concrete rack-scale AI infrastructure product aligned with NVIDIA Blackwell.
quote: introducing the world most powerful Rec solution today the Asus ESC AI part with Nvidia gb200 mvl 72

speaker: Delta keynote
announcement: Vertical power delivery for AI accelerators
summary: Delta described vertical last-lap power delivery from the bottom of AI accelerator packages to reduce trace length, voltage drop, and energy loss. The enabling technology is embedding power conversion, including magnetic components, inside the substrate.
quote: By doing something like this, we can reduce the length of the last lab power delivery trace and accordingly cuts down the voltage drop and the energy loss leading to something like 13% efficiency improvement which may seem small but is is a major deal is a major deal.

speaker: Delta keynote
summary: Delta said it has liquid cooling solutions across air-to-liquid, liquid-to-liquid, and immersion cooling for AI data centers. This positions Delta to support higher-power AI accelerators where traditional air cooling is insufficient.
quote: At this point, Delta Electronics has a liquid cooling solution in all three categories.

speaker: Delta keynote
announcement: End-to-end precision cooling solution for data centers
summary: Delta said it is working on an end-to-end precision cooling solution for entire data centers, moving beyond single cooling components. The announcement matters as AI data centers increasingly require integrated thermal management.
quote: We are also working on a endtoend precision cooling solution for the entire data center.

speaker: Delta keynote
summary: Delta introduced AI-based Wi-Fi sensing that repurposes Wi-Fi signals for device positioning by solving multipath effects. The system claims materially better accuracy than signal-strength-based Wi-Fi positioning.
quote: We use AI algorithms to to solve this multi pass problem and come up with a precise positioning system whose error is about 35 cm point 35 m which is about five to 10 times smaller than existing signal based signal strength based Wi-Fi positioning system.

speaker: Delta keynote
summary: Delta announced Wi-Fi-based sensing algorithms that estimate breathing rate, heartbeat rate, body movement, and sleep stage without wearables or EEG. The capability enables applications such as sleep monitoring and healthcare sensing using existing Wi-Fi devices.
quote: So by analyzing the minor variations in the Wi-Fi of the in the SCSI of the Wi-Fi signals, we come up with a breathing rate estimation algorithm that can achieve something like 95% accuracy at a 5 m distance as well as a hard beaten rate estimation algorithm that can achieve something like 83% accuracy at a one meter distance.

speaker: Delta keynote
summary: Delta announced Q Wi-Fi, a quality-of-service-conscious Wi-Fi system that uses AI for network-wide access point coordination, self-organization, self-optimization, and built-in device positioning. It aims to deliver private-5G-like performance at lower cost.
quote: Q Wi-Fi develop Q Q Wi-Fi builds this device positioning capability as a first class functionality into every constituent access point and is able to reduce the interapp handover latency from what used to be something like 100 to 150 millisecond down to something like less than 30 millisecond which is comparable to or better than most 5G systems and in addition with the device positioning technology can enable more effective dynamic load balancing among Wi-Fi access point and continuous network operation in even in the presence of planned or unplanned access point shutdown.

speaker: Delta keynote
summary: Delta announced BITS, an end-to-end AI automation system for drone-based inspection of large infrastructure such as bridges. It automates 3D modeling, inspection flight path computation, GPS-denied autonomous flight, image capture, defect detection, and report generation.
quote: So what's the upshot? The upshot is that bits uses this end to end automation uh capability to uh cut down the bridge inspection cost by half and reduce the elapse time to one/ird.

speaker: Delta keynote
summary: Delta positioned BITS as a level-four automation system for drone-based bridge inspection, including automated image analysis and inspection report generation. This substantially reduces operator involvement versus current manual drone inspection workflows.
quote: But with bits bits correspond to level four automation.

speaker: Delta keynote
summary: Delta announced Kota, a system that generates large numbers of corner-case simulations to stress test ADAS software and identify failures. It provides pass/fail results, pinpoints whether perception or control caused a failure, and suggests remedial training.
quote: Kota is the the test case generator that generate uh supposedly generate this millions and millions of corner test cases and then pray them in the in the virtual environment simulator.

speaker: Delta keynote
summary: Delta said Kota can generate additional training cases based on failed ADAS tests, retrain the deficient subsystem, and retest until the issue is corrected. Delta framed this closed-loop test-identify-remove capability as differentiated versus state-of-the-art ADAS testing.
quote: I claim that it is the kotas capability to completing this test identify problem remove problem test identify problem remove problem loop that uniquely pushes behind the state-of-the-art in a adas testing that is to our knowledge there's no other adas testing can do anything like this okay so eventually eventually under Kota um when um ADA system fail the more test ADA system fails the better it is going to get this is somewhat somewhat sort of like a paradox but that's what happens like the more test you can fail under kota the better you are going to get right so finally we all know that um AI technology is eating the world and the revolution is just beginning.

speaker: Delta keynote
announcement: Autonomous mobile robot that can ride legacy elevators
summary: Delta disclosed work on an autonomous mobile robot capable of using legacy elevators without human intervention. This suggests Delta is extending its AI automation portfolio into building and logistics robotics.
quote: For example, we have uh autonomous mobile robot that can ride legacy elevator that you can take take the elevators up and down without human intervention.

speaker: Delta keynote
announcement: AI-driven PCB layout tool for power electronics
summary: Delta disclosed an AI-driven PCB layout tool specifically tailored to power electronics circuit design. The tool targets automation of a specialized design workflow central to Delta’s power-electronics business.
quote: And we have a AIdriven PCB layout tool that is specifically tailored to power circuit power electronics circuit design and so on and a bunch of other things.

speaker: AI TOP launch
announcement: GIGABYTE AI TOP local AI training platform
summary: GIGABYTE announced AI TOP as a desktop local AI training solution combining hardware, software, and support tools. It targets organizations that want to fine-tune customized AI models locally while using standard electrical infrastructure.
quote: As a result the GIGABYTE AI TOP solutions are able to perform up to 2.63 token per Watts

speaker: AI TOP launch
summary: AI TOP Utility is GIGABYTE’s local LLM training software with a no-code interface, dashboard monitoring, scheduling, and expert mode. It is positioned to make local fine-tuning of open-source LLMs accessible to non-experts.
key_number: top 20 most downloaded open source LLMs
quote: Also we have tested the top 20 most downloaded open source LLMs with parameters ranging from 100MB to 236B which are fully available in the AI Top Utility

speaker: AI TOP launch
summary: AI TOP Tutor is a GIGABYTE cloud service that provides conversational guidance for configuring AI TOP hardware, software setup, and technical support. It complements AI TOP Utility by helping beginners and professionals start local AI training workflows.
quote: In summary the AI TOP Tutor is a cloud service that provides real-time conversation and leads you to the answer of every hardware and software question about doing local AI training

speaker: AI TOP launch
summary: GIGABYTE introduced the TRX50 AI TOP motherboard for multi-GPU local AI training systems. It supports up to four dual-slot GPUs, large DDR5 memory capacity, multiple M.2 SSDs, and dual 10GbE networking.
quote: The TRX 50 AI TOP provides 4 PCI Express 5.0 x16 slots to support up to 4 dual-slot graphics cards

speaker: AI TOP launch
summary: GIGABYTE introduced the W790 AI TOP motherboard as a higher-end AI training board. It offers more PCIe expansion, DDR5 R-DIMM support, six M.2 slots, and Thunderbolt 4 connectivity.
quote: This one features a similar but a little bit more premium configuration 5 PCIe 5.0 x16 slots

speaker: AI TOP launch
announcement: GeForce RTX 4070 Ti SUPER AI TOP 16G
summary: GIGABYTE announced an AI TOP version of the GeForce RTX 4070 Ti SUPER with 16GB of VRAM for local AI training builds. The company emphasized its value and ability to scale to four cards on a single 1600W PSU.
quote: Since most of computing jobs are handled by GPU we are now introducing GeForce RTX 4070 Ti SUPER AI TOP 16G

speaker: AI TOP launch
summary: GIGABYTE announced the Radeon Pro W7900 AI TOP 48G, a professional graphics card for AI TOP local training systems. It uses a two-slot turbo-fan design and ECC memory-oriented professional components and firmware settings.
quote: Sincerely introducing W7900 AI TOP 48G and W7800 AI TOP 32G

speaker: AI TOP launch
summary: GIGABYTE announced the Radeon Pro W7800 AI TOP 32G as part of its AI TOP professional GPU lineup. The card is aimed at local AI training systems requiring professional graphics features and ECC support.
quote: Sincerely introducing W7900 AI TOP 48G and W7800 AI TOP 32G

speaker: AI TOP launch
announcement: GIGABYTE AMD Radeon Pro AIB partnership
summary: GIGABYTE announced it has become a Professional Graphics Add-In-Board partner collaborating with AMD Radeon Pro. This underpins the new Radeon Pro AI TOP cards in GIGABYTE’s local AI training ecosystem.
quote: We are thrilled to announce that GIGABYTE has become the first and the only Professional Graphics Add-In-Board Partner on The Market that collaborates with AMD Radeon Pro series

speaker: AI TOP launch
summary: GIGABYTE announced AI TOP 100E SSDs in 2TB and 1TB SKUs for AI training workloads. The drives use Phison aiDAPTIVE technology to extend storage into training workflows and are positioned for much higher endurance than regular SSDs.
quote: Powered by Phison aiDAPTIVE technology these two skus have a 150-times of Total Bytes Written comparing to the regular SSD model so you don’t have to worry too much that huge amount of data exchange will kill your SSD’s lifespan

speaker: AI TOP launch
summary: GIGABYTE announced the UD1600PM PG5 AI TOP power supply for AI TOP multi-GPU systems. It is designed for AI training workloads, supports four AI TOP graphics cards, and carries an 80 Plus Platinum certification and long warranty.
quote: The PSU is also certified with 80 Plus Platinum and a 10-year warranty proven our commitment to provide the best AI product

speaker: Pat Gelsinger
summary: Intel launched Xeon 6 with Efficient-cores for high-density, scale-out data-center workloads. The product emphasizes higher core count, better performance per watt, and rack consolidation versus older Xeon generations.
quote: Today we are launching Xeon 6 with E-cores.

speaker: Pat Gelsinger
summary: Intel committed to a higher-core-count follow-on Xeon 6 E-core processor later in 2024. The 288-core version is positioned to enable greater rack consolidation for data centers.
quote: Later this year, we will be bringing the second generation of our Xeon 6 with E-cores, a whopping 288 cores.

speaker: Pat Gelsinger
