announcement: Oracle and ByteDance join Arm AGI CPU family
summary: Arm announced Oracle and ByteDance as new partners/customers for the Arm AGI CPU ecosystem. This expands validation of Arm’s server CPU push beyond the customers named at its March event.
quote: Oracle huge partner with OCI. We have a long history with Oracle. They've now joined the ARGIC CPU family as well as Bite Dance.

speaker: Rene Haas
announcement: Arm AGI CPU 2 and AGI CPU 3 roadmap
summary: Arm committed to a multigenerational AGI CPU roadmap, with AGI CPU 2 already underway and AGI CPU 3 planned. The roadmap is aligned with Arm’s compute subsystem cadence for partners and Arm’s own chips.
quote: ARM AGIC CPU 2 is already underway.

speaker: Rene Haas
announcement: Nvidia RTX Spark powered by Arm
summary: Arm highlighted Nvidia’s RTX Spark as an Arm-powered agentic AI PC platform using a custom Grace CPU paired with Blackwell GPU technology. The system is positioned to bring local agentic AI workloads to Windows-on-Arm PCs.
quote: 20 cores ARMbased cores in the custom grace CPU.

speaker: Rene Haas
summary: Nvidia said the RTX Spark PC has 128 GB of memory, enabling large compressed AI models to run locally. Jensen Huang stated that with MVFP4 compression it could support a 100-billion-parameter model on the PC.
quote: Well, if you look at the RTX Spark PC, it's got 128 gigabytes memory.

speaker: Rene Haas
summary: Nvidia described MVFP4 as a new numerical format created to compress large language models for local execution in RTX Spark-class systems. The format is intended to fit smarter AI models into system memory.
quote: And so we uh created a new uh numerical format called MVFP4 so that we can compress the large language models as much as possible and fit a very smart AI into the system memory.

speaker: Rene Haas
announcement: Adobe Photoshop and Premiere CUDA acceleration for agents
summary: Nvidia said Adobe will rearchitect Photoshop and Premiere to be CUDA-accelerated and accessible to agentic systems. This is a concrete software ecosystem commitment to make creative tools faster and controllable by AI agents.
quote: Adobe announced they're going to rearchitect Adobe Photoshop and Premiere so that it's CUDA accelerated and a aentically accessible and so we're accelerating applications.

speaker: ROG press event
summary: A compact anniversary-edition gaming mini PC with an NVIDIA RTX 5090 Laptop GPU and new removable stand for horizontal or vertical placement. It matters as ASUS is pushing flagship-class laptop GPU performance into a 3-liter desktop form factor.
quote: The ROG Knock 16 Edition 20.

speaker: ROG press event
summary: A limited anniversary full-tower gaming desktop built around AMD Ryzen 9 X3D CPUs and an ROG Astral GeForce RTX 5090 GPU. It targets the highest-end desktop gaming segment with a massive chassis and custom cooling.
quote: And for those seeking ultimate performance, the ROG G1000 edition 20 is a massive 104 LRA tower engineer for flagship hardware with the limited anniversary look and the integrated enemy hollow for unique personalization.

speaker: ROG press event
summary: A 27-inch anniversary OLED gaming monitor using tandem OLED technology, true-black glossy coating, and dual-mode high-refresh operation. It targets competitive gamers needing extremely fast response and refresh rates.
quote: And what truly sets it apart is its versatile dual mode offering crystal clear QHD at 540 hertz and incredible HD at the 720 Hz.

speaker: ROG press event
summary: An anniversary-edition flagship Wi-Fi 7 gaming router designed to prioritize game traffic for lower-latency, more stable play. It complements ASUS’s high-refresh display push with networking aimed at competitive gaming.
quote: And complementing this, the ROG Rapture GTB 98 Pro Edition 20 routers delivers flagship Wi-Fi 7 performance, prioritizing the game traffic for ultra fast, stable connection, and smooth gameplay without slowdowns.

speaker: ROG press event
summary: A premium open-frame modular aluminum PC case with multiple placement orientations and a focus on M.2 SSD thermal management. It is aimed at enthusiast DIY builders seeking high airflow and showcase flexibility.
quote: Starting with the RG GR20 Edition 20.

speaker: ROG press event
summary: A 3000W high-end power supply using server-grade GaN MOSFET components and GPU-focused voltage stabilization. It is positioned for extreme multi-GPU and overclocking builds, including up to four RTX 5090 GPUs.
quote: That's why we proud to introduce the RG Thor 3000 watt Titanium 3 edition 20 3000 W.

speaker: ROG press event
summary: An anniversary-edition graphics card with a curved AMOLED display, liquid metal thermal interface, quad-fan cooling, and BTF connector support. It extends ASUS’s flagship RTX 5090 offering with higher-end cooling and customization features.
quote: And for those who demand the ultimate visual experience from these extreme system, we are thrilled to unveil the ROG Astro GeForce RTX 1590 edition 20.

speaker: ROG press event
summary: A 20th-anniversary flagship AMD motherboard with enhanced power delivery, extensive M.2 support, copper VRM cooling, and collectible design elements. It anchors ASUS’s new extreme DIY PC ecosystem.
quote: And to bring all these incredible components together and form the very foundation of your extreme system, we proudly present the ROG Crosshair X870E edition 20.

speaker: ROG press event
summary: A dedicated anniversary AIO liquid cooler paired with the Crosshair X870E Edition 20, using an Asetek Gen 10 V3 RX pump and cableless AIOQ connector. It is meant to deliver an integrated high-end cooling solution for ASUS’s flagship DIY platform.
quote: In addition, this motherboard is paired with ROG new Gene 360 edition 20 as its dedicated cooling solution right out of the sandbox.

speaker: ROG press event
announcement: Tandem RGB OLED monitor GaN power and Neo Proximity upgrades
summary: ASUS is rolling out upgraded proximity-sensor controls and gallium-nitride power supplies across its full tandem RGB OLED monitor lineup. The GaN power design reduces waste heat, supporting longer OLED lifespan without performance compromises.
quote: Now, both of those upgrades, the proximity sensor and the new gallium nitride power, those ship across our full tandem RGB OLED lineup.

speaker: ROG press event
summary: A new larger 32-inch sibling in ASUS’s tandem RGB OLED monitor lineup. It will be available in early Q3 alongside the previously announced PG27UCWM.
quote: That includes the PG27 UCWM that we announced at CES, as well as its new larger sibling that we're announcing today, the 32 in PG32 UCWM.

speaker: ROG press event
summary: A 24-inch Full HD tandem WOLED esports monitor with true-black glossy coating and 540Hz refresh rate. ASUS positions it as the world’s first OLED esports monitor aimed directly at professional competitive players.
quote: We are incredibly proud to announce the ROG Stricks OLED XG259 QWPG Ace, the world's first OLED esports monitor, 24 in full HD and a tandem W OLED display featuring our true black glossy coating and a staggering 540 Hz refresh rate.

speaker: ROG press event
summary: ASUS’s 2026 flagship gaming laptop with Intel Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus, NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 Laptop GPU, redesigned cooling, and a new 4K 240Hz mini-LED display. It is the company’s highest-power laptop yet for esports and hardcore gaming.
quote: So, we made our new Strict Scar 18 capable of up to 320 watts of total sustained system power that drives an Intel Core Ultra 9 processor 290HX Plus and an Nvidia GeForce RTX 5090 laptop GPU.

speaker: ROG press event
summary: ASUS launched a dedicated ROG Esport Global Instagram channel for team status, real-time tournament information, and esports gear content. It is a concrete community and marketing commitment tied to ASUS’s esports push.
quote: So, alongside all this gear, we're also excited to announce that our new ROG Esport Global Instagram channel is now online to provide the status of your favorite teams, real-time tournament info, and of course, the best ROG esports gear.

speaker: ROG press event
summary: A new anniversary handheld gaming device with a larger OLED display, TMR joysticks, transforming D-pad, redesigned buttons, and AMD Ryzen AI Z2 Extreme processor. It updates ASUS’s handheld gaming platform with higher-end display and control hardware.
quote: The analyze display has been expanded from 7 to 7.4 4 in and upgraded to a stunning OLED panel, delivering truly breathtaking vibrant HDR visual.

speaker: ROG press event
summary: Anniversary-edition gaming AR glasses designed to pair with the ROG Xbox Ally X20, using micro-OLED displays, 3DoF tracking, and single-cable connectivity. It creates a portable virtual-screen experience integrated with the Ally command center.
quote: Whether you are gaming at home or on the go, the R1 steps up your immersion with a 171 ines virtual screen just a 4 meters away, covering 95% of your focus field of view and delivering a near instantaneous 0.01 millisecond response time thanks to micro OLED.

speaker: Global Press Conf
summary: Computex 2026 is being positioned as a full-stack AI ecosystem event spanning AI computing, robotics, mobility, next-gen tech, startups, and forums. The stated scale underscores Taiwan’s role as a convening hub for the AI hardware and systems supply chain.
quote: This year, we are proud to host more than 1,500 exhibitors across 6,000 booths spanning Nankang Exhibition Center, the World Trade Center, and the Taipei International Convention Center.

speaker: Global Press Conf
summary: Computex is adding its first dedicated robotics pavilion, highlighting physical AI as a major focus area. The pavilion is intended to showcase robotics and drone applications from multiple technology vendors.
quote: And this year, for the first time Computex dedicates an entire pavilion to robotics.

speaker: Global Press Conf
summary: Nvidia will host GTC Taipei as part of the Computex 2026 venue footprint. This creates a dedicated Nvidia developer and ecosystem event alongside Computex’s broader AI hardware conference.
quote: In Nangang Exhibition Hall 1, Hall 2, the Taipei World Trade Center, and also at the Taipei International Convention Center, where Nvidia will host GTC Taipei.

speaker: Global Press Conf
summary: Computex will host CEO keynotes from Qualcomm, Marvell, Intel, and NXP, covering AI computing from edge devices to data-center connectivity and physical AI. This is a concrete conference-program announcement rather than a product launch.
quote: Our CEO keynotes bring four global chip leaders to one stage.

speaker: Global Press Conf
summary: Computex is creating a concentrated physical-AI forum track with senior robotics executives from Nvidia, Qualcomm, ABB, and NXP. The program highlights robotics and embodied AI as a dedicated theme at the show.
quote: Right inside that same building on the morning of June the 2nd four forum sections take the stage in a row all on physical AI with the senior executives running robotics at Nvidia, Qualcomm, ABB, and NXP.

speaker: Global Press Conf
summary: InnoVEX will feature nearly 500 startups from 23 countries focused on AI applications including robotics, healthcare, materials, and industrial intelligence. The scale signals a broadened AI startup ecosystem presence at Computex.
quote: And at InnoVex, nearly 500 startups from 23 countries are showing where AI goes next in robotics, health care, materials, and industrial intelligence.

speaker: Global Press Conf
summary: Computex will extend its programming into Taipei with a live-streamed Taipei 101 drone show using Taiwanese-made drones. This is a concrete event commitment tied to showcasing Taiwan-made robotics/drone capability.
quote: Tomorrow night, with Taipei 101 as the international landmark stage, thousand MIT drones will light up the night sky, bringing together technology and art for audience around the world through a live streaming.

speaker: Enter Infinity
announcement: D5 DX memory technology with CQD support
summary: Gigabyte announced D5 DX memory technology for its Intel Z890 refresh motherboards, using CQD to support high memory capacity without sacrificing speed. The stated headline is up to 8,000 MT/s while supporting 256 GB of system memory.
quote: With CQD, we achieve speeds up to 8,000 MT per second while supporting 256 GB of system memory.

speaker: Enter Infinity
summary: Gigabyte introduced the X870 Aorus Infinity motherboard for AMD Ryzen X3D systems, emphasizing very tight memory timings and high memory speed. It is positioned as a premium AMD platform board for gaming and overclocking.
quote: Introducing the X70 Orus Infinity.

speaker: Enter Infinity
summary: Gigabyte announced its most advanced motherboard, using 3D metal printed cooling structures and a high-phase power design. The board is positioned as an extreme-performance platform with data-center-inspired thermals and power delivery.
quote: By combining the MOSFET and choke into a single module, each units delivers the equipment of four power faces that give us a staggering 64 total phases.

speaker: Enter Infinity
summary: Gigabyte announced a wood-themed X870 Aero Aorus Darkwood motherboard, extending its design-focused consumer motherboard lineup. The product targets users building a coordinated wood-accent PC ecosystem.
quote: Introducing the XA70 Echo Arrow Acrody Darkwood.

speaker: Enter Infinity
summary: Gigabyte announced a wood-accented Aorus GeForce RTX 5080 Infinity Wood graphics card. It pairs the RTX 5080 with Gigabyte's Windforce Hyperblast cooling system and matching wood design language.
quote: To complete the wood theme ecosystem, I'm thrilled to announce Orus GeForce RTS 5080 Infinity Wood.

speaker: Enter Infinity
announcement: B850M Aorus Stealth and B850M Aorus Stealth Ice
summary: Gigabyte introduced the B850M Aorus Stealth and B850M Aorus Stealth Ice as reverse-connector MicroATX gaming motherboards. The announcement expands Gigabyte's cable-hidden motherboard ecosystem into MATX.
quote: We have partnered with over 20 top tiers manufacturers ensuring out ofbox compatability with more than 50 different models.

speaker: Enter Infinity
summary: Gigabyte announced Aorus Elite AIO liquid coolers with a latest-generation dual-chamber pump, single-frame radiator fans, edge-to-edge lighting, and an onboard display. The coolers add monitoring for CPU, pump, VRAM, and memory data when paired with Gigabyte components.
quote: Introducing the ORS Elite AIO coolers.

speaker: Enter Infinity
summary: Gigabyte announced the Aorus P1600 AI Top, a compact flagship PSU for local AI desktops and multi-GPU systems. It supports up to four graphics cards with native 16-pin PCIe cables and carries 80 Plus Titanium efficiency.
quote: Again, that's why we engineer the OSB 1600 AI talk, our flagship 1600 watt PSU built for the next gen AI systems.

speaker: Enter Infinity
summary: Gigabyte announced the Aorus C510 Glass Infinity, a compact MicroATX case with an integrated gaming display and flexible orientation. It is designed for portable, clean-layout AI and gaming builds with reverse-connector motherboard support.
quote: Introducing Orus C510 Glass Infinity, a very compact 25 L MicroATX case, and it's a transformer.

speaker: Enter Infinity
summary: Gigabyte announced the Aorus K10 Infinity gaming keyboard with Hall-effect magnetic switches, per-key actuation programming, an OLED touchscreen, and onboard performance analytics. It targets high-end gamers seeking low latency and configurable input behavior.
quote: And it is not just precise, it is fast.

speaker: Enter Infinity
summary: Gigabyte announced the Aorus M10 Infinity mouse with optical switches, Nvidia Reflex compatibility, and a hybrid aluminum-magnesium alloy base. The mouse is positioned for low-latency competitive gaming with premium materials.
quote: The M10 utilize optical switches up to 8,000 Hz poly rate also and the compatibility with Nvidia reflex.

speaker: Enter Infinity
summary: Gigabyte introduced Gmaz Web Edition as a browser-based, software-free control center for customizing its peripherals. Settings and stats are stored locally on the keyboard and mouse for portability across systems.
quote: So to complete the peripherals here we introduce Gmaz web edition a software free control center all the customized settings and stats that we've just mentioned are stored locally on the keyboard and mouse you just need to open the link on your browser then adjust everything directly so wherever you go your setup goes with you now let's jump back to the systems and talk about what truly drive performance these days.

speaker: Enter Infinity
announcement: Aorus GeForce RTX 5080, 5070 Ti and 5070 Infinity graphics cards
summary: Gigabyte expanded the Aorus Infinity graphics card lineup beyond the flagship with RTX 5080, RTX 5070 Ti, and RTX 5070 models. The cards use Windforce cooling and a stealth cable design for cleaner builds.
quote: Introducing the Orus GeForce 5080 Infiniti joined by the 5070Ti and 5070 Infiniti that comes later as part of the 4 series.

speaker: Enter Infinity
announcement: GPU Selector for Aorus AI Box
summary: Gigabyte announced GPU Selector software for its Aorus AI Box external GPU products, assigning applications across multiple GPUs based on real-time performance. The tool is aimed at reducing GPU resource conflicts in gaming, streaming, content creation, and local AI workloads.
quote: This could be a substantial leap efficiency and responsiveness especially in demanding use cases like gaming while streaming content creation or running local AI workloads simultaneously and in an optimized scenarios you can boost up to five time faster performance compared to a default unadjust settings.

speaker: Enter Infinity
summary: Gigabyte introduced the AI Top 100, a local AI supercomputer for developers, researchers, and teams building AI agents. It combines high-end desktop CPUs and GPUs with large memory and local deployment to run models on-premises without cloud dependency.
quote: AMD Ryzen 9 9950X, Nvidia GeForce RTX 5090 or AMD Radian AI Pro are 9700s up to 128 GB of DDR5i memory, 1600 W 80 plus certificate power supply, support for local AI models up to 200 billion parameters.

speaker: Enter Infinity
summary: Gigabyte announced the Aorus Elite gaming monitor series, including 27-inch and 32-inch tandem OLED models with QHD and UHD options. The lineup emphasizes premium display performance, tactical gaming features, and selected extended warranty coverage.
quote: The lineup are with both QHD and UHD resolutions with refresh rate ranging from 240 Hz up to 540.

speaker: Enter Infinity
announcement: Aorus Elite multi-mode 5K mini LED gaming monitor
summary: Gigabyte announced a multi-mode 5K mini LED gaming monitor for the Aorus Elite series. It supports switching among 5K, 4K, and QHD modes and uses high local dimming density for improved contrast and reduced haloing.
quote: Furthermore, we are also very excited to introduce the world's first multi mode 5K mini LED gaming monitor with a glassy display to our Aurus Elite series.

speaker: Enter Infinity
announcement: AI OLED Care Pro with four-year burn-in warranty
summary: Gigabyte announced AI OLED Care Pro, an automated OLED protection system that performs background usage monitoring and pixel cleaning. Selected Aorus Elite OLED models will include burn-in warranty coverage of up to four years.
quote: Therefore, today we are proud to announce that selected models, including the FO27Q28G, come with an extended warranty that covers burning for over up to 4 years.

speaker: Enter Infinity
announcement: NVMP4 quantization integration in Gmate Creator
summary: Gigabyte announced integration of Nvidia NVMP4 quantization into its Gmate Creator software. The feature is presented as improving large language model performance and efficiency on Gigabyte AI PCs.
quote: Through our collaboration with Nvidia, we have integrated the new MVMP4 quantization into our GMA creator.

speaker: Enter Infinity
summary: Gigabyte announced the Gaming A16 laptop powered by Intel Core Ultra 9 and high-TGP graphics in a slim chassis. The product targets portable gaming and AI workloads with improved performance efficiency.
quote: The brand new Gigabyte Gaming A16 delivers a major leap in performance.

speaker: Enter Infinity
summary: Gigabyte announced the Aorus Master 16, a thin 16-inch gaming laptop using AMD's Ryzen 9 9955HX3D CPU and Nvidia RTX 5090 Laptop GPU. It targets desktop-class gaming and AI performance in a 19 mm chassis.
quote: We proudly announce our brand new Orals Master 16.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
summary: Intel said its 18A process is now at full scale and is supporting a full product lineup with hundreds of design wins. This matters because 18A is the manufacturing foundation Intel is using to reassert process competitiveness across client, edge and data-center products.
quote: The Intel 18A process is now at full scale.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
announcement: Intel Arc G3 handheld gaming GPU
summary: Arc G3 is a tuned high-performance GPU derived from Core Ultra Series 3 for handheld gaming devices. Intel claimed materially better performance and power versus competition, with devices available later in the month.
quote: We are more than 40% faster, 40% faster, and at the same performance, we're half the power.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
summary: Intel is taking its latest Series 3 products into edge markets across multiple verticals. The announcement extends client silicon into edge AI use cases such as manufacturing, robotics and retail.
quote: And that's why this year, we're taking our latest series 3 products into the edge business with over 130 designs in multiple verticals.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
announcement: Perplexity hybrid agentic inference on Core Ultra Series 3
summary: Intel and Perplexity announced hybrid agentic inference that runs smaller local models on the Core Ultra Series 3 GPU while orchestrating cloud models for non-private tasks. This positions Intel client silicon as part of an AI-agent privacy and cost architecture.
quote: And so for the first time ever, we work together to create hybrid agenic inference.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
summary: Xeon 6 Plus is a new Intel data-center CPU built on Intel 18A with dense E-core compute and large L3 cache. Intel positioned it for cloud, network infrastructure and agentic AI workloads where CPU density and efficiency matter.
quote: That is why we are excited to have Intel Xeon 6 Plus introduced at the Computex this week.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
summary: Intel said ODM partners are bringing Xeon 6 Plus server and rack solutions to market immediately. This indicates ecosystem readiness beyond the CPU announcement itself.
quote: Moreover, our ODM partners are bringing Zeon 6 plus solutions to the market today.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
summary: Intel launched a rack-scale blueprint initiative for intelligent infrastructure based on open standards. The first examples target agentic performance with Xeon 6 P-cores and agent density with Xeon 6 Plus E-cores.
quote: That is why we start the initiative called Rex scale blueprints.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
summary: Intel and Foxconn announced work on rack-scale products built on Intel Xeon processors. The partnership targets differentiated rack-scale AI infrastructure solutions for diverse AI workloads.
quote: Intel and Foxcom are working together to develop Rex scale products built upon Intel Xeon processors.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
announcement: SambaNova SM50 rack-scale AI infrastructure with Xeon 6
summary: SambaNova said the SM50 rack-scale AI infrastructure combines Intel Xeon 6 processors with SambaNova SM50 RDUs for agentic workloads. The system is slated to ship to customers later in the year.
quote: It uses Intel Xeon 6 processors with Sonova SM50 RDUs and shipping to customers later this year.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
summary: Intel and SambaNova demonstrated a heterogeneous disaggregated inference stack using Intel CPUs, SambaNova RDUs and Nvidia GPUs. The companies claimed the architecture can reduce end-to-end latency and outperform GPU-only inference in tests.
quote: So the initial result of our work is disagregated inference is the the GPUs, the RDUs, the CPUs that's the fastest and artificial analysis and our test found to be two to three times faster than just the GPUs alone.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
announcement: Vector Core Compute VC2 disaggregated inference neocloud
summary: Vista Equity Partners and Cambium Capital launched Vector Core Compute VC2, described as a commercially available architecture for disaggregated inference. The effort targets conversion of existing data centers into inference data centers, with Together AI named as first commercial customer.
quote: The demo you just witnessed with Rodrigo was conducted live in our Los Angeles data center and we have over 50 deployments planned in the US which are targeted to convert existing data centers to inference data centers.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
summary: Intel announced it has officially entered the purpose-built/custom silicon market. The effort extends Intel IP and design capabilities to external customers beyond Intel’s own products.
quote: I was just thinking what better place than Computex and Taipei where custom silicon really is the name of the game here to announce that Intel has officially entered this market.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
summary: Intel disclosed a partnership with Google under which Intel is delivering an infrastructure processing unit for hyperscaler workloads. Intel said the silicon is already designed and being deployed.
quote: Google and Intel have gone into a partnership wherein Intel is delivering what is called as the infrastructure processing unit.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
summary: Intel said Ericsson chose Intel to deliver next-generation infrastructure silicon at global scale for telecom markets. This is a custom silicon win in Intel’s purpose-built silicon business.
quote: Ericson has been uh partnering with us and Erikson chooses us wherein we deliver or Intel delivers the next generation infrastructure silicon for at a global scale for them across the board.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
summary: Intel and Echo Neurotechnologies announced work on brain-trained algorithms for streaming speech. The collaboration targets more efficient AI and potential speech-restoration tools for people who have lost speech.
quote: Together, we're developing brain trained algorithms for streaming speech that approach the efficiency of biological computation.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
announcement: Greenstone Biosciences life-science imaging reference architectures
summary: Intel announced a partnership with Greenstone Biosciences to establish scalable reference architectures for life-science imaging and biomedical AI workloads. The collaboration combines Greenstone human biology data with Intel AI computing.
quote: We are partnering with Greenstone to establish scalable reference architectures applicable across the broader life science imaging ecosystem.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
announcement: Hitachi collaboration on foundry tools and quantum computing systems
summary: Intel highlighted Hitachi as a partner for foundry tools and quantum computing systems. The announcement frames Hitachi’s industrial capabilities as complementary to Intel advanced computing.
quote: Another key partners is Hitachi.

speaker: Lip-Bu Tan
summary: Intel and Siemens expanded their partnership across chip design, manufacturing and chip applications in Siemens products. The collaboration includes EDA automation, manufacturing software, product lifecycle management, automation, electrification, quality and sustainability.
quote: We are expanding our partnership across the entire value chain from design to manufacturing to chip applications in seammen's products.

speaker: CEO keynote
summary: Marvell said it will sample a 1.6 terabit coherent optical solution built on 2nm for long-distance data-center interconnect. This matters because coherent optics are required for AI scale-across links spanning hundreds to thousands of kilometers.
quote: And later this year we'll be sampling the world's first 1.6 terabit, 2 nanometer coherent optical solution.

speaker: CEO keynote
summary: Marvell announced a new 100T Ethernet switch aimed at AI data centers, claiming industry-lowest power. It expands Marvell’s AI data-center switching portfolio at a higher aggregate bandwidth tier.
quote: And today we announced our new 100 to T Ethernet switch specifically designed for AI data centers with the industry's lowest power.

speaker: CEO keynote
summary: Marvell showed a co-packaged optics switch using a 51.2T switch die surrounded by sixteen 3.2T optical engines. It demonstrates Marvell’s move to bring optical connectivity directly to the package to overcome copper power and density limits.
quote: In this case, this is our 51.2 T switch and all around the edges are 16 3.2 T optical engines.

speaker: CEO keynote
summary: Marvell highlighted an expanded strategic partnership with Nvidia spanning optics, photonics and NVLink Fusion, alongside Nvidia’s $2 billion investment in Marvell. The partnership ties Marvell connectivity technologies into Nvidia’s AI infrastructure ecosystem and heterogeneous data-center architecture.
quote: And as part of this announcement that we made few months back in video invested two billion dollars into Marvall.

speaker: CEO keynote
announcement: ASE capacity preparation for Marvell AI infrastructure products
summary: ASE said it is preparing manufacturing capacity for Marvell, reflecting a concrete supply-chain commitment to support Marvell’s expected AI infrastructure growth. This matters because optical and advanced packaging ramps require ecosystem capacity ahead of deployment.
quote: So we're busy now preparing the capacity for you.

speaker: Jensen Huang
announcement: NVIDIA DSX AI factory infrastructure platform
summary: NVIDIA DSX is an AI-factory reference design and operations platform spanning simulation, provisioning, power/cooling optimization, and grid interaction. It matters because NVIDIA is extending from chips and systems into full AI infrastructure design and operations.
quote: NVIDIA DSX is the blueprint: a reference design for building and operating AI factories at maximum efficiency and profitability.

speaker: Jensen Huang
summary: Vera Rubin is NVIDIA’s multi-rack, pod-scale agentic AI supercomputer platform spanning GPUs, Vera CPUs, BlueField storage/security, NVLink, and Spectrum-X networking. NVIDIA said it is now in full production, indicating the platform has moved beyond roadmap status.
quote: Vera Rubin is in full production.

speaker: Jensen Huang
summary: Vera Rubin NVL72 is the GPU rack-scale subsystem for agentic AI thinking workloads such as prompt/context understanding, reasoning, and planning. It is part of the broader Vera Rubin pod-scale architecture.
quote: The Vera Rubin GPU — six trillion transistors with over 18,000 components on one board.

speaker: Jensen Huang
summary: Vera is NVIDIA’s new custom data-center CPU architecture built for agentic AI orchestration, tool use, memory movement, and low-latency interaction with GPUs. NVIDIA positions it as a major new growth driver and a CPU designed for agents rather than human-centric cloud workloads.
key_number: 1.8 times the agentic sandbox performance of x86 CPUs
quote: Vera delivers 1.8 times the agentic sandbox performance of x86 CPUs.

speaker: Jensen Huang
summary: The Vera CPU rack is a liquid-cooled rack-scale CPU system for orchestrating agentic AI workloads, memory movement, and tool launching. It is part of the Vera Rubin multi-rack system.
key_number: 256 CPUs in a single liquid-cooled rack
quote: Then, the Vera CPU rack — 256 CPUs in a single liquid-cooled rack, orchestrating the models, shuffling memory, launching tools.

speaker: Jensen Huang
announcement: Vera LPX rack with Groq LPUs
summary: Vera LPX is a low-latency inference rack using Groq LPUs, complementary to Vera Rubin NVL72’s high-throughput token generation. It targets ultra-low-latency token generation in agentic systems.
key_number: 40 petabytes per second of SRAM bandwidth
quote: Foxconn and Quanta brought the Vera LPX rack to shape — 256 Groq LPUs across 16 trays, 40 petabytes per second of SRAM bandwidth — for ultra-low latency.

speaker: Jensen Huang
summary: Vera BlueField-4 STX is NVIDIA’s storage processing and security system for AI agent memory, combining compute, networking, storage acceleration, and in-silicon security. It is positioned as a critical-path component for agentic AI storage and context handling.
quote: Vera BlueField-4 STX — where AI keeps its memory.

speaker: Jensen Huang
summary: NVIDIA announced Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics, an Ethernet switch using co-packaged optics. It is intended for AI factory scale-out networking within Vera Rubin infrastructure.
quote: And NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics — the world’s first Ethernet switch with 200-gigabit co-packaged optics.

speaker: Jensen Huang
announcement: NVIDIA Agent Toolkit for Enterprise AI
summary: NVIDIA announced an enterprise agent toolkit spanning models, harnesses, tools/skills, and runtime to help companies build and deploy AI agents. The toolkit includes OpenShell as a secure enterprise harness/runtime and is optimized for NVIDIA AI platforms across cloud, on-prem, and devices.
quote: And so, we have the NVIDIA Agent Toolkit for Enterprise AI.

speaker: Jensen Huang
summary: OpenShell is NVIDIA’s secure enterprise shell for running agents with policy, privacy, identity, and privilege controls. NVIDIA said it is open source and being adopted by major enterprise software platforms.
quote: And so this OpenShell is being adopted all over the world, and it is open source.

speaker: Jensen Huang
summary: Cadence and NVIDIA are partnering on chip design agents that use Codex, Cadence Chip Stack, Nemotron, OpenShell, Xcelium, and JasperGold to accelerate RTL verification and debug. The commitment targets a major EDA workflow and shows agentic AI being applied to semiconductor design productivity.
quote: Verification cycles over 40 times faster.

speaker: Jensen Huang
summary: Nemotron 3 Ultra is NVIDIA’s next open model for enterprise agents, released with model, training scripts, and data. It uses a hybrid SSM and Mixture-of-Experts architecture to improve speed and cost for reasoning and tool-use workloads.
quote: Today, we’re announcing Nemotron 3 Ultra.

speaker: Jensen Huang
summary: RTX Spark is a new NVIDIA AI PC platform/chip for agentic Windows PCs, combining a Blackwell RTX GPU, custom Grace CPU, NVLink, and unified memory. It is built with MediaTek and Microsoft to run CUDA, Windows applications, and local or cloud-connected AI agents.
quote: Introducing RTX Spark — everything we’ve learned over 33 years, distilled into one chip.

speaker: Jensen Huang
summary: NVIDIA announced RTX Spark laptops as the first AI-agent PC form factor based on the RTX Spark platform. The laptops are positioned as Windows-compatible, CUDA-capable machines for local agent workflows and accelerated applications.
quote: Ladies and gentlemen, NVIDIA RTX Spark laptops.

speaker: Jensen Huang
announcement: Adobe Photoshop and Premiere for RTX Spark
summary: Adobe is re-engineering Photoshop and Premiere for RTX Spark and adding agent-friendly MCP server support. NVIDIA said the RTX Spark release will double performance for these applications.
quote: They have re-engineered the architecture, the core of Adobe Photoshop and Premiere, and they’ll release it for RTX Spark.

speaker: Jensen Huang
summary: NVIDIA and Microsoft announced a new line of three Windows-compatible agentic PCs spanning laptop, desktop, and workstation form factors. NVIDIA framed it as a new product family with a roadmap across every future architecture generation.
quote: And today we’re announcing a whole new line — three revolutionary Windows machines, covering desktop, laptop, and workstation — all 100% Windows compatible, 100% CUDA, 100% NVIDIA AI Tensor Core.

speaker: Jensen Huang
summary: DGX Station is a Windows-compatible desktop workstation for large language model and agent developers. It is positioned as the high-end workstation member of the new NVIDIA-Microsoft PC line, capable of running trillion-parameter models locally.
quote: And if you want an incredible system — this is DGX Station.

speaker: Jensen Huang
summary: Cosmos 3 is NVIDIA’s open frontier model system for physical AI, robotics, simulation, and world/action modeling. NVIDIA says it can understand, reason, generate, simulate, and serve as policy support for robots and other physical AI systems.
quote: And today, we’re announcing Cosmos 3.

speaker: Jensen Huang
summary: Alpamayo 2 Super is an open model for self-driving cars, announced as part of NVIDIA’s physical AI and automotive stack. The transcript states NVIDIA is working with car companies globally around DRIVE Hyperion vehicles.
quote: Today we’re announcing Alpamayo 2 Super, an open model for self-driving cars.

speaker: CEO keynote
summary: NXP introduced a three-layer edge-AI architecture for physical AI and robotics, separating reasoning, coordination, and reflex control. The architecture is meant to place intelligence close to sensors and actuators to improve latency, safety, and energy efficiency.
quote: Intelligence has three layers.

speaker: CEO keynote
announcement: eIQ Toolkit for VLA edge deployment
summary: NXP positioned its eIQ toolkit as software for bringing vision-language-action and other AI models from the cloud to constrained edge processors by importing, quantizing, pruning, compiling, and tuning them for target hardware. This matters because it lowers friction for deploying physical AI on edge devices.
quote: This is a software that allows to grab those models, imports them, quantize and prune them, compile it.

speaker: CEO keynote
summary: NXP disclosed work with Boston Dynamics combining NXP hardware and software with Boston Dynamics robotics platforms for human-robot cooperation in factories. The collaboration targets physical-AI deployment on factory floors.
quote: I mean we're very proud of working with one of our customers here.

speaker: CEO keynote
announcement: GE Healthcare intelligent anesthesia systems engagement
summary: NXP disclosed an engagement with GE Healthcare to deploy intelligent systems in anesthesia applications where precision and safety are critical. This indicates NXP edge-AI silicon and software are being used in healthcare physical-AI systems.
quote: And we're doing I'm very proud of the engagement that we have with GE Healthcare where they're actually deploying intelligent systems and anesthesia where precision safety really really makes a difference and their products and XP is at the core of it.

speaker: Cristiano Amon
summary: Qualcomm announced Dragonfly as the new product brand for its data center products, extending its portfolio from edge devices into data center compute. This matters because it signals Qualcomm’s concrete branding and commercialization push for AI data center products.
quote: So today at Computex, we're announcing the new product brand for Qualcomm data center products.

speaker: Cristiano Amon
announcement: Dragonfly hyperscaler and global partner deployments
summary: Qualcomm said it is already working with hyperscalers and global partners on real-world deployments for its data center products. This indicates customer and ecosystem engagement beyond a concept-stage announcement.
quote: Um, we're already working with hyperscalers and global partners on real world deployments.

speaker: Cristiano Amon
announcement: Open Claw and Hermes on Snapdragon
summary: Qualcomm said orchestrators Open Claw and Hermes are now running on Snapdragon, positioning Snapdragon devices as endpoints for agentic AI workflows. This matters because orchestration software is a key layer for distributed AI across devices and cloud.
quote: Orchestrators like Open Claw and Hermes are now running on Snapdragon.

speaker: Cristiano Amon
announcement: Claude Desktop native on Snapdragon PCs
summary: Qualcomm said agentic assistants such as Claude Desktop are running natively on Snapdragon PCs. This supports Qualcomm’s push to make Snapdragon PCs a platform for local agentic AI workloads.
quote: Aentic assistants like claw desktop are running native natively on Snapdragon PCs and you see this thing only accelerating and how the users are going to use more and more in their machines and the machines going to be running on their own doing things and cloud platforms for example like perplexity computer are now building orchestration layers that connect to the device.

speaker: Cristiano Amon
announcement: Perplexity orchestration layers connected to Snapdragon devices
summary: Qualcomm said Perplexity is building orchestration layers that connect to the device, aligning cloud AI platforms with Snapdragon edge compute. This is relevant to Qualcomm’s distributed AI thesis across cloud and device.
quote: Aentic assistants like claw desktop are running native natively on Snapdragon PCs and you see this thing only accelerating and how the users are going to use more and more in their machines and the machines going to be running on their own doing things and cloud platforms for example like perplexity computer are now building orchestration layers that connect to the device.

speaker: Cristiano Amon
announcement: Google Gemini agentic AI in Android
summary: Qualcomm said Google is bringing agentic AI directly into Android using Gemini intelligence. This matters because Android is a major Snapdragon device ecosystem and native agentic AI could drive future device upgrade cycles.
quote: Google is bringing Agentic AI directly into Android with Gemini intelligence.

speaker: Cristiano Amon
announcement: Microsoft agentic AI on Snapdragon ecosystem
summary: Qualcomm said Microsoft is also bringing agentic AI capabilities in the same context as Google’s Android/Gemini integration. This supports the broader Snapdragon PC and device ecosystem for agentic workloads.
quote: Microsoft's doing the same and partners even like Humane are developing full Aentic operating system running on Snapdragon.

speaker: Cristiano Amon
announcement: Humane agentic operating system on Snapdragon
summary: Qualcomm said Humane is developing a full agentic operating system running on Snapdragon. This indicates a concrete Snapdragon-based software platform effort for agent-centric devices.
quote: Microsoft's doing the same and partners even like Humane are developing full Aentic operating system running on Snapdragon.

speaker: Cristiano Amon
summary: Qualcomm described a comprehensive robotics platform spanning AMRs, industrial arms, quadrupeds, humanoids and drones. This matters because it frames Qualcomm’s edge AI hardware and software stack as a multi-form-factor robotics platform rather than a single robot product.
quote: What we're building is a comprehensive platform across different form factors from AMR, industrial arms, four-legged robots, humanoids, drones, and I actually think industrial opportunity is happening right now.

speaker: Charles Liang
summary: Supermicro said its next-generation Intel-, AMD-, NVIDIA- and Arm-based system and rack solution portfolios are ready for OEM orders. This matters because it expands the company’s building-block server lineup across the major AI silicon ecosystems.
quote: we have our X14 H14 uh portfolio in bodium deployment since many years ago and now we have a X15 uh Intia CPUbased H15 uh AMD uh processor base and uh N15 Nvidia CPU GPUbased solution and a new one A15 AM base uh system and Rex solution.

speaker: Charles Liang
summary: Supermicro said it is preparing high-volume production of NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 rack-scale systems and has already shipped samples to select customers. This is a concrete rack-scale AI infrastructure commitment tied to NVIDIA’s next-generation platform.
quote: we are getting ready for the upcoming Vera Rubin uh MVL 72 right indeed we already ship uh some sample to uh certain customer and so far their experience was great and we are preparing for high volume production for you to to order today

speaker: Charles Liang
summary: Supermicro announced Rubin HGX configurations supporting up to 144 GPUs, with samples around now and production expected in the next few months. This broadens Supermicro’s NVIDIA AI server offering beyond NVL72 into multiple rack-scale HGX configurations.
quote: Then uh lubin hgx right lubin uh hgx we support uh a non uh 64 GPU or non or can be uh with another different configuration right kind of like this congulation uh uh we can support uh uh 64 uh 128 or 144 uh R2 200 uh system to you and uh sample is about now and uh production just in next few months

speaker: Charles Liang
summary: Supermicro said NVIDIA Vera CPU-based systems with multiple configurations are already around sampling. This points to Supermicro support for NVIDIA’s CPU-centric data-center platform in addition to GPU systems.
quote: and also uh Vera uh CPU base right uh looping base and Vera base right vera based you know CPU uh Nvidia CPU based uh again kind of lots of different configuration uh already about simply

speaker: Charles Liang
summary: Supermicro and Arm said Supermicro systems using Arm’s AGI CPU are ready to take orders and expected to enter production within a few months. This gives enterprises and neoclouds access to Arm-based AI infrastructure outside hyperscaler-only deployments.
quote: We we're going to be in uh uh production within within a few months here.

speaker: Charles Liang
announcement: 5,000-pound AI rack with BBU power shelf
summary: Supermicro highlighted its own rack design for Vera Rubin-class systems, including optimized battery power shelves and optional battery backup systems. The rack is designed to support very heavy AI infrastructure and improve installation, maintenance and resilience.
quote: And even this rack, you can see this rack is so strong.

speaker: Charles Liang
summary: Supermicro announced liquid-to-air cooling modules for running direct liquid cooling in traditional air-cooled data centers without major conversion work. The stated capacities include 250 kW and 500 kW, aimed at faster enterprise AI deployment.
quote: You just uh have a uh a to a liquid to a cycle car um 250 kw uh 500 kw and then you can run your DLC direct in traditional uh data center right away without spend what uh months of time to uh uh convert your data center to install a DLC equipment and to spend the money, right?

speaker: Charles Liang
summary: Supermicro announced a broader CDU portfolio spanning megawatt-scale cooling and smaller in-row units. The portfolio is intended to support dense AI workloads with pre-validated liquid-cooling infrastructure.
quote: So uh let's talk about cooling right again we provide all different kind of CDU right kind of uh uh in those CDU uh which different size uh 1 megawatt 3 megawatt or 5 megawatt or kind of like a uh indirect CPU indeed micro is one of the first company uh design and manufacturer in low CPU right no CDU kind of uh 125 mega uh 125 kw 250 kw or even a little bit larger size is coming right

speaker: Charles Liang
summary: Supermicro introduced a new coolant chemistry for direct liquid cooling with much higher electrical impedance than standard coolant. The goal is to reduce outage and short-circuit risk if a small leak occurs.
quote: Uh our new coolant we have a some new coolant formula that feature up to 1,000 time higher electrical impedance than standard coolant for example here.

speaker: Charles Liang
summary: Supermicro said it recently introduced a 1.2 terabit co-packaged optics optical switch for massive-scale compute clusters. The switch is positioned to reduce communication bottlenecks with lower power, lower latency and higher port density.
quote: Recently we also introduced our 1.2 two terabit CPO optical switch uh engineered specifically to remove communication bottleneck in massive scale uh computer cluster while delivering ultra low power lowest latency and unmatch port density.

speaker: Charles Liang
summary: Supermicro announced a liquid-cooled 800G and 1.6 terabit switching platform for dense AI workloads. It is nearly ready for sampling, with production targeted by the stated end-of-period timing.
quote: Furthermore, to support our thermal uh realities of next generation dense AI workload, we are delivering a full liquid core 800G and 1.6 six terabit sheet platform all about uh almost ready for sampling and we can start production by end of GCE to eliminate a traditional third party uh integration delay.

speaker: Charles Liang
summary: Supermicro announced its own enhanced industry-standard SONiC networking operating system, offered in both community-supported and enterprise-supported versions. This helps Supermicro pair networking software directly with its AI cluster hardware stack.
quote: repair our hardware with our own enhanced industry uh standard sonic networking operating system supporting both the community contribution and our full supported enterprise version.

speaker: Charles Liang
summary: Supermicro announced a unified cluster-management software suite including SuperCloud Composer, Director, Automation Center and Developer Console. The suite provides single-pane management, telemetry, power monitoring and anomaly detection across very large AI data-center deployments.
quote: So after your choice on top of all the networking layer we provide an intelligent software state right our super cluster software suite that including our super cloud composer uh super cloud director supercloud automation center uh super cloud developer console and other software that give you uh Unify a single pane management platform across your data center.

speaker: Charles Liang
announcement: Expanded global on-site deployment and lifecycle support
summary: Supermicro committed to expanded on-site service, deployment teams, lifecycle support and local depots for AI data-center customers. The commitment is meant to reduce activation time and downtime risk for rack-scale AI deployments.
quote: We don't just ship product.

speaker: Charles Liang
summary: Supermicro announced that its global capacity has expanded to six million square feet, including recent additions in San Jose and Taiwan. This supports the company’s commitment to ship large AI rack and data-center deployments at scale.
quote: So uh even uh three years ago uh our capacity only about two to three million square feet but today global we have six million square feet.
